title: Airphoto Selection Pipeline
text: fly_footprint() converts point centroids rectangular polygons representing estimated ground coverage. standard 9” x 9” negative produces footprint width 9 * scale * 0.0254 metres.
code: footprints <- fly_footprint(centroids) plot(st_geometry(aoi), col = "lightyellow", border = "grey40", main = "Photo footprints") plot(st_geometry(footprints), border = "steelblue", add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(centroids), pch = 20, cex = 0.5, col = "red", add = TRUE)

title: Airphoto Selection Pipeline
text: fly_filter() method = "footprint" catches photos whose centroid falls outside AOI whose footprint overlaps — common situation large-scale photos edge study area.
code: fp_result <- fly_filter(centroids, aoi, method = "footprint") ct_result <- fly_filter(centroids, aoi, method = "centroid") cat("Footprint method:", nrow(fp_result), "photos\n") #> Footprint method: 20 photos cat("Centroid method: ", nrow(ct_result), "photos\n") #> Centroid method:  7 photos

title: Airphoto Selection Pipeline
text: fly_summary() reports footprint dimensions date ranges scale.
code: fly_summary(centroids) #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   scale   photos footprint_m half_m year_min year_max #>   <chr>    <int>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <int>    <int> #> 1 1:12000     10        2743   1372     1968     1968 #> 2 1:31680     10        7242   3621     1968     1968

title: Airphoto Selection Pipeline
text: fly_coverage() computes percentage AOI covered photo footprints, grouped column.
code: fly_coverage(centroids, aoi, by = "scale") #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   scale   n_photos covered_km2 coverage_pct #>   <chr>      <int>       <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 1:12000       10        15.1         60.7 #> 2 1:31680       10        24.8        100

title: Airphoto Selection Pipeline
text: fly_select() picks minimum set photos needed reach target coverage, using greedy set-cover algorithm iteratively selects photo covering uncovered area.
code: selected <- fly_select(centroids, aoi, target_coverage = 0.80) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Selecting photos (target: 80% coverage)... #>   3 photos -> 81.6% coverage #> Selected 3 of 20 photos for 81.6% coverage #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on selected[, c("airp_id", "scale", "selection_order", "cumulative_coverage_pct")] #> Simple feature collection with 3 features and 4 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.6796 ymin: 54.41035 xmax: -126.5269 ymax: 54.46049 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #>    airp_id   scale selection_order cumulative_coverage_pct #> 11  697358 1:31680               1                    41.0 #> 20  697329 1:31680               2                    66.5 #> 12  697292 1:31680               3                    81.6 #>                          geom #> 11 POINT (-126.6796 54.41035) #> 20 POINT (-126.6039 54.42617) #> 12 POINT (-126.5269 54.46049) sel_fp <- fly_footprint(selected) plot(st_geometry(aoi), col = "lightyellow", border = "grey40",      main = paste(nrow(selected), "photos selected (greedy)")) plot(st_geometry(sel_fp), border = "steelblue", col = adjustcolor("steelblue", 0.15), add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(selected), pch = 20, col = "red", add = TRUE)

title: Airphoto Selection Pipeline
text: practice often want photos best available resolution, backfill uncovered area coarser scales. combines fly_select() priority loop scales.
code:
sf_use_s2(FALSE) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off scales_priority <- c("1:12000", "1:31680") target_coverage <- 0.80  aoi_albers <- st_transform(aoi, 3005) |> st_union() |> st_make_valid() aoi_area <- as.numeric(st_area(aoi_albers)) selected_all <- NULL remaining_aoi <- aoi_albers  for (i in seq_along(scales_priority)) {   sc <- scales_priority[i]   photos_sc <- centroids[centroids$scale == sc, ]   if (nrow(photos_sc) == 0) next    if (i == 1) {     # Best resolution: take ALL photos     cat(sc, ": taking all", nrow(photos_sc), "photos
")     sel <- photos_sc     sel$selection_order <- seq_len(nrow(sel))     sel$cumulative_coverage_pct <- NA_real_   } else {     # Coarser scales: greedy select on remaining uncovered area     remaining_sf <- st_sf(geometry = st_geometry(remaining_aoi)) |>       st_transform(4326) |> st_make_valid()     sel <- fly_select(photos_sc, remaining_sf, target_coverage = target_coverage)   }    if (nrow(sel) > 0) {     fp <- fly_footprint(sel) |> st_transform(3005)     fp_union <- st_union(fp) |> st_make_valid()     remaining_aoi <- tryCatch(       st_difference(remaining_aoi, fp_union) |> st_make_valid(),       error = function(e) remaining_aoi     )     covered_pct <- 1 - as.numeric(st_area(remaining_aoi)) / aoi_area     cat("  -> ", nrow(sel), " photos at ", sc,         " (cumulative: ", round(covered_pct * 100, 1), "%)
")     sel$priority_scale <- sc     selected_all <- rbind(selected_all, sel)   } } #> 1:12000 : taking all 10 photos #>   ->  10  photos at  1:12000  (cumulative:  60.7 %) #> Selecting photos (target: 80% coverage)... #>   5 photos -> 87.7% coverage #> Selected 5 of 10 photos for 87.7% coverage #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on #>   ->  5  photos at  1:31680  (cumulative:  95.2 %)  cat("
Total:", nrow(selected_all), "photos
") #>  #> Total: 15 photos as.data.frame(table(selected_all$priority_scale)) #>      Var1 Freq #> 1 1:12000   10 #> 2 1:31680    5 sel_fp <- fly_footprint(selected_all) plot(st_geometry(aoi), col = "lightyellow", border = "grey40",      main = paste(nrow(selected_all), "photos (priority selection)")) # Color by scale cols <- ifelse(selected_all$priority_scale == "1:12000", "steelblue", "darkorange") border_cols <- cols fill_cols <- adjustcolor(cols, 0.15) for (j in seq_len(nrow(sel_fp))) {   plot(st_geometry(sel_fp[j, ]), border = border_cols[j],        col = fill_cols[j], add = TRUE) } legend("topright", legend = scales_priority,        fill = adjustcolor(c("steelblue", "darkorange"), 0.3),        border = c("steelblue", "darkorange"), bty = "n")

what: Airphoto Footprint Estimation and Coverage Selection
title: Airphoto Footprint Estimation and Coverage Selection
text: Select minimum set historic airphotos needed cover study area.

title: Airphoto Footprint Estimation and Coverage Selection
text: Historic airphotos essential documenting landscape change, ordering tedious — ’re choosing thousands overlapping frames across multiple scales decades. Photo centroids available BC Data Catalogue knowing frames actually cover area interest requires estimating ground footprints scale film format. fly estimates footprints, filters actual ground coverage (just centroids), picks smallest set meets target. Best-resolution photos first, coarser scales fill gaps.  Neexdzii Kwa (Morice River) floodplain near Houston, BC — 1968 photos 1:12,000 (blue) 1:31,680 (orange).

title: Airphoto Footprint Estimation and Coverage Selection
code: pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/fly")

title: Airphoto Footprint Estimation and Coverage Selection
code: library(fly) library(sf)  # Estimate footprint rectangles from centroids footprints <- fly_footprint(centroids)  # Filter photos whose footprint overlaps the AOI filtered <- fly_filter(centroids, aoi, method = "footprint")  # Select minimum set for 95% coverage selected <- fly_select(filtered, aoi, target_coverage = 0.95)

title: Airphoto Footprint Estimation and Coverage Selection
text: flooded delineates floodplain extents DEMs stream networks — use generate AOI polygons fly selects photos .

title: Airphoto Footprint Estimation and Coverage Selection
text: Full walkthrough priority selection airphoto selection vignette.

what: fly: Airphoto Footprint Estimation and Coverage Selection — fly-package
title: fly: Airphoto Footprint Estimation and Coverage Selection — fly-package
text: Estimate ground footprints airphoto centroids scale, compute coverage areas interest, select minimum photo sets using greedy set-cover. Includes optional helpers querying fish habitat streams bcfishpass.

title: fly: Airphoto Footprint Estimation and Coverage Selection — fly-package
text: Maintainer: Allan Irvine al@newgraphenvironment.com (ORCID) contributors: New Graph Environment [copyright holder]

what: Check photo coverage of an AOI by group — fly_coverage
title: Check photo coverage of an AOI by group — fly_coverage
text: Builds footprint polygons photo, intersects AOI, reports percent coverage grouped column.

title: Check photo coverage of an AOI by group — fly_coverage
code: fly_coverage(photos_sf, aoi_sf, by = "photo_year")

title: Check photo coverage of an AOI by group — fly_coverage
text: photos_sf sf point object scale column. aoi_sf sf polygon check coverage . Column name group (default "photo_year").

title: Check photo coverage of an AOI by group — fly_coverage
text: tibble grouping column, n_photos, covered_km2, coverage_pct.

title: Check photo coverage of an AOI by group — fly_coverage
code: centroids <- sf::st_read(system.file("testdata/photo_centroids.gpkg", package = "fly")) #> Reading layer `photo_centroids' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/fly/testdata/photo_centroids.gpkg'  #>   using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 20 features and 9 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.7631 ymin: 54.34512 xmax: -126.449 ymax: 54.47635 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 aoi <- sf::st_read(system.file("testdata/aoi.gpkg", package = "fly")) #> Reading layer `aoi' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/fly/testdata/aoi.gpkg' using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 0 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.75 ymin: 54.34813 xmax: -126.45 ymax: 54.47 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 fly_coverage(centroids, aoi, by = "scale") #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   scale   n_photos covered_km2 coverage_pct #>   <chr>      <int>       <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 1:12000       10        15.1         60.7 #> 2 1:31680       10        24.8        100

what: Filter photos by spatial relationship with an AOI — fly_filter
title: Filter photos by spatial relationship with an AOI — fly_filter
text: Subsets photos whose footprint centroid intersects area interest. footprint method catches photos whose centroid falls outside AOI whose ground coverage overlaps .

title: Filter photos by spatial relationship with an AOI — fly_filter
code: fly_filter(photos_sf, aoi_sf, method = c("footprint", "centroid"), buffer = 0)

title: Filter photos by spatial relationship with an AOI — fly_filter
text: photos_sf sf point object scale column. aoi_sf sf polygon defining area interest. method One "footprint" (default) "centroid". buffer Buffer distance metres added AOI testing intersection (default 0). Applied BC Albers (EPSG:3005).

title: Filter photos by spatial relationship with an AOI — fly_filter
text: subset photos_sf intersects AOI.

title: Filter photos by spatial relationship with an AOI — fly_filter
code: centroids <- sf::st_read(system.file("testdata/photo_centroids.gpkg", package = "fly")) #> Reading layer `photo_centroids' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/fly/testdata/photo_centroids.gpkg'  #>   using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 20 features and 9 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.7631 ymin: 54.34512 xmax: -126.449 ymax: 54.47635 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 aoi <- sf::st_read(system.file("testdata/aoi.gpkg", package = "fly")) #> Reading layer `aoi' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/fly/testdata/aoi.gpkg' using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 0 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.75 ymin: 54.34813 xmax: -126.45 ymax: 54.47 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 # Footprint method finds more photos than centroid method fp_result <- fly_filter(centroids, aoi, method = "footprint") ct_result <- fly_filter(centroids, aoi, method = "centroid") nrow(fp_result) >= nrow(ct_result) #> [1] TRUE

what: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
text: Creates rectangular polygons representing estimated ground coverage airphoto, based film negative dimensions reported scale.

title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
code: fly_footprint(centroids_sf, negative_size = 9)

title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
text: centroids_sf sf point object scale column (e.g. "1:31680"). negative_size Negative dimension inches (default 9 standard 9" x 9").

title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
text: sf polygon object CRS input, footprint rectangles.

title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
text: Ground coverage computed negative_size * scale_number * 0.0254 metres per side. Rectangles constructed BC Albers (EPSG:3005) accurate metric distances, transformed back input CRS.

title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
code: centroids <- sf::st_read(system.file("testdata/photo_centroids.gpkg", package = "fly")) #> Reading layer `photo_centroids' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/fly/testdata/photo_centroids.gpkg'  #>   using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 20 features and 9 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.7631 ymin: 54.34512 xmax: -126.449 ymax: 54.47635 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 footprints <- fly_footprint(centroids) plot(sf::st_geometry(footprints))

what: Query bcfishpass for habitat streams — fly_query_habitat
title: Query bcfishpass for habitat streams — fly_query_habitat
text: Queries bcfishpass.streams_vw table stream segments modelled habitat (rearing spawning) given species watershed group.

title: Query bcfishpass for habitat streams — fly_query_habitat
code: fly_query_habitat(   conn,   wsgroup,   habitat_type = "rearing",   species_code = "co",   blue_line_keys = NULL,   stream_names = NULL,   min_stream_order = NULL )

title: Query bcfishpass for habitat streams — fly_query_habitat
text: conn DBI connection bcfishpass database. wsgroup Watershed group code (e.g. "BULK", "LNIC"). habitat_type "rearing" "spawning". species_code Species code: "co", "ch", "sk", "bt", "st", "wct", "cm", "pk". blue_line_keys Numeric vector FWA blue_line_key values (preferred — unique per stream). stream_names Character vector GNIS stream names (convenience — scoped wsgroup). min_stream_order Minimum Strahler order (applied addition blk/name filters).

title: Query bcfishpass for habitat streams — fly_query_habitat
text: sf linestring object WGS84 (EPSG:4326).

what: Query FWA lake polygons that intersect habitat streams — fly_query_lakes
title: Query FWA lake polygons that intersect habitat streams — fly_query_lakes
text: Returns lake polygons whse_basemapping.fwa_lakes_poly share waterbody_key input streams. Use fly_trim_habitat() fill gaps lakes interrupt stream network.

title: Query FWA lake polygons that intersect habitat streams — fly_query_lakes
code: fly_query_lakes(conn, streams_sf)

title: Query FWA lake polygons that intersect habitat streams — fly_query_lakes
text: conn DBI connection bcfishpass database. streams_sf sf linestring — habitat streams (e.g. fly_query_habitat()).

title: Query FWA lake polygons that intersect habitat streams — fly_query_lakes
text: sf polygon object WGS84 (EPSG:4326), NULL waterbody keys found.

what: Select minimum photo set to cover an AOI (greedy set cover) — fly_select
title: Select minimum photo set to cover an AOI (greedy set cover) — fly_select
text: Iteratively picks photo whose footprint covers uncovered area target coverage reached.

title: Select minimum photo set to cover an AOI (greedy set cover) — fly_select
code: fly_select(photos_sf, aoi_sf, target_coverage = 0.95)

title: Select minimum photo set to cover an AOI (greedy set cover) — fly_select
text: photos_sf sf point object scale column (pre-filtered target year/scale). aoi_sf sf polygon cover. target_coverage Stop fraction reached (default 0.95).

title: Select minimum photo set to cover an AOI (greedy set cover) — fly_select
text: sf object (subset photos_sf) added columns selection_order cumulative_coverage_pct.

title: Select minimum photo set to cover an AOI (greedy set cover) — fly_select
code: centroids <- sf::st_read(system.file("testdata/photo_centroids.gpkg", package = "fly")) #> Reading layer `photo_centroids' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/fly/testdata/photo_centroids.gpkg'  #>   using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 20 features and 9 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.7631 ymin: 54.34512 xmax: -126.449 ymax: 54.47635 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 aoi <- sf::st_read(system.file("testdata/aoi.gpkg", package = "fly")) #> Reading layer `aoi' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/fly/testdata/aoi.gpkg' using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 0 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.75 ymin: 54.34813 xmax: -126.45 ymax: 54.47 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 selected <- fly_select(centroids, aoi, target_coverage = 0.80) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Selecting photos (target: 80% coverage)... #>   3 photos -> 81.6% coverage #> Selected 3 of 20 photos for 81.6% coverage #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on selected[, c("airp_id", "scale", "selection_order", "cumulative_coverage_pct")] #> Simple feature collection with 3 features and 4 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.6796 ymin: 54.41035 xmax: -126.5269 ymax: 54.46049 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #>    airp_id   scale selection_order cumulative_coverage_pct #> 11  697358 1:31680               1                    41.0 #> 20  697329 1:31680               2                    66.5 #> 12  697292 1:31680               3                    81.6 #>                          geom #> 11 POINT (-126.6796 54.41035) #> 20 POINT (-126.6039 54.42617) #> 12 POINT (-126.5269 54.46049)

code: centroids <- sf::st_read(system.file("testdata/photo_centroids.gpkg", package = "fly")) #> Reading layer `photo_centroids' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/fly/testdata/photo_centroids.gpkg'  #>   using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 20 features and 9 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.7631 ymin: 54.34512 xmax: -126.449 ymax: 54.47635 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 fly_summary(centroids) #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   scale   photos footprint_m half_m year_min year_max #>   <chr>    <int>       <dbl>  <dbl>    <int>    <int> #> 1 1:12000     10        2743   1372     1968     1968 #> 2 1:31680     10        7242   3621     1968     1968

what: Trim floodplain to areas alongside target streams — fly_trim_habitat
title: Trim floodplain to areas alongside target streams — fly_trim_habitat
text: Uses flat-cap buffer extend perpendicular streams without extending past stream endpoints. Optionally includes lake polygons fill gaps lakes interrupt stream network. Optionally adds photo capture buffer.

title: Trim floodplain to areas alongside target streams — fly_trim_habitat
code: fly_trim_habitat(   floodplain_sf,   streams_sf,   lakes_sf = NULL,   floodplain_width = 2000,   photo_buffer = 1800 )

title: Trim floodplain to areas alongside target streams — fly_trim_habitat
text: floodplain_sf sf polygon — floodplain lateral habitat boundary. streams_sf sf linestring — pre-filtered streams (fly_query_habitat() source). lakes_sf sf polygon — lake polygons include (fly_query_lakes() source). Fills gaps lakes interrupt stream networks. NULL skip. floodplain_width Buffer distance (m) perpendicular streams. capture full floodplain width. Uses flat end caps. photo_buffer Buffer (m) around trimmed floodplain photo centroid capture. Set 0 return trimmed floodplain .

title: Trim floodplain to areas alongside target streams — fly_trim_habitat
text: sf polygon WGS84 (EPSG:4326).

title: Trim floodplain to areas alongside target streams — fly_trim_habitat
code: streams <- sf::st_read(system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "fly")) #> Reading layer `streams' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/fly/testdata/streams.gpkg'  #>   using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 10 features and 5 fields #> Geometry type: LINESTRING #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.7081 ymin: 54.35513 xmax: -126.5041 ymax: 54.47 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 floodplain <- sf::st_read(system.file("testdata/floodplain.gpkg", package = "fly")) #> Reading layer `floodplain' from data source  #>   `/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/fly/testdata/floodplain.gpkg'  #>   using driver `GPKG' #> Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 0 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -126.7588 ymin: 54.31208 xmax: -126.4191 ymax: 54.48792 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 trimmed <- fly_trim_habitat(floodplain, streams, photo_buffer = 0) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Buffering streams by 2000m (flat cap)... #> Intersecting with floodplain... #> Error in st_area.sfc(result): package lwgeom required, please install it first #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on plot(sf::st_geometry(trimmed)) #> Error: object 'trimmed' not found

title: fly (development version)
text: Initial release. Airphoto footprint estimation coverage selection, extracted airbc.
